pattern: Partition a contiguous job list into exactly d non-empty days with DP; each day's cost is its maximum job difficulty, and we minimize the total across all valid partitions.
hints: If there are fewer jobs than days, it is impossible — you cannot put at least one job on every day. Return -1 immediately.

Define dp[i][j] as the minimum schedule cost using exactly i days to schedule the first j jobs. Transition: try all split points k in [i-1, j-1] — assign jobs k..j-1 to day i and carry forward dp[i-1][k].

The inner transition scans from right to left, tracking the running max of jobDifficulty[k..j-1] as k decreases. This avoids a redundant max-recomputation and keeps the overall complexity at O(n^2 * d).

edge_inputs.description: one day — cost is the max of all jobs
all jobs in first day except last
all zeros — minimum cost is 0 per day
identical difficulties — every partition costs d * value
optimal split avoids the largest peak

stress.description: random job list near max length, random d
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    d = rng.randint(1, min(n, 10))
    jobs = [rng.randint(0, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [jobs, d]
